metric_comparisons.metric: M*_0 agreement: eigenbasis-route vs direct-matrix-closed-form (Section 5.3 vs 5.6)

parametric.evidence: Not specified anywhere in the paper (continuous-time throughout); terminal_price_error empirically shrinks as n_steps grows (0.13 at 100 steps -> 0.0026 at 20000 steps), consistent with an Euler-Maruyama discretization artifact rather than an implementation bug.
parametric.suggested_fix: Increase --config training.n_steps for tighter terminal-price agreement if higher precision is needed; does not affect any of the four closed-form identity checks, which use independent fixed quadrature grids.

parametric.hyperparameter: sigma(t) functional form for back_pedersen1998, m(t)/nu for cdf2016, C/sigma eigenvalues for common_eigenbasis
parametric.assumed_value: illustrative synthetic choices (sin-wave sigma(t); constant m=0.1, nu=0.2; random SPD C)
parametric.evidence: The paper states these cases hold for ARBITRARY deterministic sigma(t) / m(t) / shared-eigenbasis (C, sigma); specific numeric choices are needed only to run a concrete demo and do not correspond to any paper-reported configuration (none exists, since this is a theory paper).
parametric.suggested_fix: None needed for the paper's own claims (which are about arbitrary members of these classes); swap in any other valid (C, sigma) if project-specific numbers are needed.

omission.missing_component: Section 5.5 general scalar stochastic-liquidity BSDE solver
omission.sir_location: SIR mathematical_spec 'Stochastic maximum principle Hamiltonian (scalar stochastic liquidity)', implementation_assumptions[1]
omission.suggested_fix: Implement a quadratic BSDE solver (deep BSDE / Han-Jentzen-E or LSMC) for eq. (5.44)-(5.45) if this case needs to be covered; currently only Sections 5.1, 5.2, 5.4, 5.6 are implemented.
None available -- this is an open problem in the source paper itself, not an omission introduced by this pipeline. fbsde_stub.py documents this and raises NotImplementedError by design.

review_reasons: overall_sir_confidence (0.58) is below the pipeline's 0.65 threshold
Section 5.5 and 5.7 paper claims are unmatched by design (out of scope / explicitly open problem), not because of an implementation failure

There is no independent human-run experiment to compare against -- these are self-verification checks of the generated code against the paper's own closed-form formulas, since the paper reports no numerical results table
